projects.title: RTS Multi-Agent Deep Q-Learning
projects.tagline: From-scratch multi-agent reinforcement learning in a custom real-time-strategy environment.
projects.imageAlt: Training reward and loss curves for the Farmers and Fighters multi-agent DQN, showing reward increasing and loss decreasing across episodes.
projects.description: A capstone reinforcement-learning project for a Deep and Reinforcement Learning course. Brady built “Farmers & Fighters,” a custom real-time-strategy environment where two teams each field a farmer agent (harvests crops to buff its fighter) and a fighter agent (eliminates the opposing team). Agents learn purely from reward signals using a hand-built training loop.
highlights: Multi-layer perceptron Q-network implemented in PyTorch
Experience replay buffer and target-network style updates
Custom environment with stochastic spawns, timed crop respawns, and a center buff
Hand-tuned reward shaping and hyperparameters with logged training curves
Reproducible setup: requirements.txt, virtual-env instructions, and demo GIFs in the repo

projects.title: Flask Advancing AI — Full-Stack Forum
projects.tagline: Secure full-stack web application: OAuth, role-based access control, and a REST API.
projects.description: A production-shaped Flask forum platform built to demonstrate full-stack web-application engineering. It combines Google OAuth 2.0 sign-in, five-tier role-based access control, salted SHA-256 password hashing, signed expiring tokens for account flows, a follow system, a Markdown post editor with sanitization, and a paginated token-authenticated REST API. Security headers and HTTPS are enforced with Flask-Talisman, and the app ships with unit tests and a Dockerfile.
highlights: Google OAuth 2.0 authentication plus custom salted SHA-256 hashing
Role-based access control across User, Editor, Moderator, Admin, and Applicant roles
Token-based confirm / reset / email-change flows with itsdangerous
Paginated REST API with Flask-HTTPAuth token authentication
Flask-Talisman security headers, unit tests, and containerized deploy
projects.demoNote: The live demo depends on a database and Google OAuth backend, so the interactive demo action is intentionally disabled on this static site.

projects.title: Training Management Calendar
projects.tagline: Flask scheduling app with filtering and CSV / PDF export.
projects.description: A lightweight scheduling tool for creating, editing, and exporting events, inspired by military training-management workflows. It pairs a Flask + SQLAlchemy backend with a FullCalendar frontend, supports authentication, category and section filters, and date-range selection, and exports events to CSV and PDF.
highlights: Full CRUD for events with authentication via Flask-Login
Filter the calendar by category, section, and date range
Export to CSV and to PDF (WeasyPrint with a wkhtmltopdf fallback)
FullCalendar frontend wired to a Flask-WTF form layer
projects.demoNote: The live demo depends on a Flask backend and database, so the interactive demo action is intentionally disabled on this static site.

projects.title: Marine Mental Strength (M2S) — AI Triage System Design
projects.tagline: Academic system-design report for a privacy-first, RAG-grounded mental-health triage assistant.
projects.context: Academic design proposal (Machine Learning coursework). Not a deployed clinical product and not built on any real patient data — it is a use-case and architecture study. Included here to show responsible-AI, privacy, and ML system-design thinking.
projects.description: A machine-learning system-design report proposing a privacy-first assistant to help reduce stigma and connect service members to mental-health care. The design centers on a retrieval-augmented-generation pipeline with a dual-encoder retriever, metadata filtering, and reranking; a primary conversational LLM; and a secondary transformer acting as a safety governor to rewrite or block unsafe outputs. It documents HIPAA Safe Harbor de-identification, NER-based PII/PHI scrubbing, consent-driven data handling, and governance under the NIST AI Risk Management Framework. The report explicitly frames the tool as triage-only, never a diagnosis, and emphasizes human clinical oversight.
highlights: RAG architecture: dual-encoder bi-transformer retriever, metadata filter, reranker
Layered safety: a secondary transformer governor blocks or rewrites unsafe outputs
Privacy engineering: HIPAA Safe Harbor de-identification and synthetic-data replacement
Governance: NIST AI RMF, red-teaming, IRB collaboration, and consent-based retention
Responsible framing: triage-only, not a diagnostic tool, with clinician oversight

projects.title: ShopSphere — Scalable Database Architecture
projects.tagline: Relational + warehouse database design for a distributed e-commerce platform.
projects.description: A database-systems design study for “ShopSphere,” a hypothetical e-commerce startup. The document works through a full data architecture: a snowflake warehouse schema, an ETL pipeline mirroring fact and dimension tables, row-level concurrency control in PostgreSQL, parameterized-query API integration, role-based access with SSL/TLS and hashing, and a horizontal-scaling plan using read replicas and partitioning.
highlights: Snowflake warehouse schema with fact/dimension separation and surrogate keys
ETL pipeline for near-real-time analytics without loading the transactional DB
Row-level locking (SELECT … FOR UPDATE) for concurrency control
Parameterized-query API layer to prevent SQL injection
Distributed scaling: read replicas, table partitioning, and load balancing
docSummary: ShopSphere: Designing a Scalable Solution and Secure Database Architecture — Brady Finnell, CSCI360 Introduction to Database Systems.

To build the ShopSphere database for analytics and efficient retrieval, a snowflake architecture is used. Tables connect through strategic primary and foreign keys, and columns are indexed by usage frequency for updates and analytics. A fact-and-dimension structure allows the database to be distributed as needed, with per-query commits managing concurrency of replicated data across server locations and authorization gating who can perform which actions.

ShopSphere is an e-commerce platform aiming to change how consumers find, research, and purchase products. The snowflake schema supports efficient analytics without sacrificing transactional speed. The goal is a system that is efficient, scalable, maintains data integrity, and keeps user data safe — covering concurrency control, data integrity, warehousing, web-platform integration, security, and scalability in a distributed environment.

Concurrency control prevents data loss when records are updated simultaneously. ShopSphere uses PostgreSQL row-level locking with SELECT … FOR UPDATE, holding a record until commit or rollback. Optimistic concurrency with timestamps was considered less reliable under heavy simultaneous transactions. Data integrity is enforced through primary and foreign keys and PostgreSQL's built-in check functions.

To keep analytics from straining the transactional server, a separate warehouse uses a snowflake schema. An Extract-Transform-Load sequence mirrors fact and dimension tables, normalizes formats, removes redundancy, and uses surrogate keys for consistency over time. Derived measures — such as sales totals per order and repeat-customer flags — are computed during transformation, and dimensions load before facts to respect dependencies.

The database communicates with the website through a secret-key-secured API. Backend services translate HTTP requests into parameterized SQL queries and stored procedures, protecting against SQL injection. The API reads from dimension and fact tables, commits transactions, and updates stock. This modular design lets the schema evolve with little frontend impact, and caching on high-traffic endpoints such as product search reduces query load.

Security uses role-based access control so only assigned roles — administrators, analysts, or API services — can perform specific actions. Sensitive data and passwords are hashed, transport is encrypted with SSL/TLS, and scheduled off-site backups plus comprehensive audit trails support recovery and compliance with standards such as GDPR and CCPA.

For scalability, ShopSphere is built for distributed deployment: read replicas increase availability for analytics and customer requests, large tables are partitioned by date or region, and application-tier load balancing spreads traffic. Together these strategies keep the system efficient and available as the customer base grows.

Through a carefully designed framework — concurrency management, thorough ETL, secure web integration, and forward-looking security and scalability — the ShopSphere database is positioned to support both current operations and future analytics and growth.
